title: Create 'databases' and 'sigma1' Kubernetes Namespaces
description: Create the 'databases' namespace for infrastructure components and the 'sigma1' namespace for application services.
details: Use `kubectl create namespace databases` and `kubectl create namespace sigma1` to provision the required Kubernetes namespaces.
test_strategy: Verify both 'databases' and 'sigma1' namespaces exist using `kubectl get ns databases sigma1`.

title: Set up Cloudflare Tunnel for Morgan Agent
description: Establish a Cloudflare Tunnel for initial connectivity for the 'morgan' service, pointing to a placeholder service or internal IP for initial connectivity.
details: Create a Cloudflare Tunnel, configure its ingress rules to point to a placeholder Kubernetes service (e.g., a simple Nginx deployment in the `sigma1` namespace, `http://nginx-placeholder.sigma1.svc.cluster.local`), and ensure the `cloudflared` client is running and connected.
test_strategy: Verify the tunnel is active and reachable from the internet using `cloudflared tunnel status` and by attempting to access the configured public hostname, confirming it serves the placeholder Nginx content.

title: Provision 'sigma1-assets' S3/R2 Bucket and Generate Access Credentials
description: Provision an S3/R2 compatible bucket named 'sigma1-assets' and generate the necessary access key ID and secret access key for programmatic access.
details: Use Cloudflare Terraform to create the 'sigma1-assets' bucket. As part of the Terraform output or a subsequent Cloudflare API call, generate an API token with read/write permissions specifically for this bucket. Securely retrieve the generated access key ID and secret access key.
test_strategy: Verify the 'sigma1-assets' bucket exists via Cloudflare R2 dashboard or CLI. Use the generated credentials with `rclone` or `aws s3` CLI to perform a test upload and download to the 'sigma1-assets' bucket, verifying read/write access.

title: Deploy CloudNative-PG Operator
description: Deploy the CloudNative-PG operator into the 'databases' namespace to enable declarative management of PostgreSQL instances.
details: Apply the CloudNative-PG operator YAML manifests or use its Helm chart to deploy it into the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pods are running and healthy.
test_strategy: Verify the CloudNative-PG operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloudnative-pg`.

title: Deploy Redis Operator
description: Deploy a Redis operator into the 'databases' namespace to manage Redis/Valkey instances.
details: Apply the Redis operator YAML manifests or use its Helm chart to deploy it into the 'databases' namespace. Ensure the operator pods are running and healthy. For example, the Spotahome Redis Operator can be used.
test_strategy: Verify the Redis operator pod is running in the 'databases' namespace using `kubectl get pods -n databases -l app=redis-operator` (adjust label as per actual operator deployment).

title: Deploy 'sigma1-postgres' Cluster
description: Deploy a single-instance PostgreSQL cluster named 'sigma1-postgres' in the 'databases' namespace, configured with persistent storage (50Gi) and an initial 'sigma1' database and 'sigma1_user'.
details: Create a `Cluster` Custom Resource for CloudNative-PG, specifying a single replica, 50Gi persistent storage, and initial database 'sigma1' with user 'sigma1_user'. Deploy this CR into the 'databases' namespace.
test_strategy: Verify 'sigma1-postgres' pods are running and healthy in the 'databases' namespace. Connect to the PostgreSQL instance using `psql` from within the cluster to confirm 'sigma1' database and 'sigma1_user' exist and are accessible.

title: Deploy 'sigma1-valkey' Cluster
description: Deploy a single-instance Redis/Valkey cluster named 'sigma1-valkey' in the 'databases' namespace using the `valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine` image.
details: Create a `Redis` (or equivalent) Custom Resource for the Redis operator, specifying a single replica and the `valkey/valkey:7.2-alpine` image. Deploy this CR into the 'databases' namespace.
test_strategy: Verify 'sigma1-valkey' pods are running and healthy in the 'databases' namespace. Connect to the Valkey instance using `redis-cli` from within the cluster to confirm accessibility and basic functionality.

title: Create Kubernetes Secret for S3/R2 Credentials
description: Create a Kubernetes Secret in the 'sigma1' namespace to securely store the sensitive S3/R2 access key ID and secret access key.
details: Create a Kubernetes `Secret` resource named `sigma1-s3-credentials` in the `sigma1` namespace. Populate it with the `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` obtained from the S3/R2 provisioning step. Ensure the secret is base64 encoded.
test_strategy: Verify the `sigma1-s3-credentials` Secret exists in the `sigma1` namespace using `kubectl get secret sigma1-s3-credentials -n sigma1 -o yaml`. Decode and inspect its content to confirm the presence and correctness of `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`.

title: Create 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ConfigMap
description: Create a ConfigMap named 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' in the 'sigma1' namespace, containing non-sensitive connection strings for PostgreSQL, Redis, S3/R2 bucket name, and Cloudflare Tunnel hostname.
details: Construct a Kubernetes `ConfigMap` named `sigma1-infra-endpoints` in the `sigma1` namespace. Populate it with `POSTGRES_URL` (e.g., `postgres://sigma1_user:password@sigma1-postgres.databases.svc.cluster.local:5432/sigma1`), `REDIS_URL` (e.g., `redis://sigma1-valkey.databases.svc.cluster.local:6379`), `S3_BUCKET_NAME` (`sigma1-assets`), and the Cloudflare Tunnel hostname. Ensure URLs are correctly formatted for service consumption.
test_strategy: Verify the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap exists in the `sigma1` namespace using `kubectl get cm sigma1-infra-endpoints -n sigma1 -o yaml`. Inspect its content to ensure all required keys are present and values are correctly formatted. From a test pod in `sigma1` namespace, attempt to `ping` or `curl` the PostgreSQL and Redis endpoints to confirm network accessibility.

title: Review Core Infrastructure Setup
description: Conduct a comprehensive review of all deployed core infrastructure components, configurations, and the `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap and `sigma1-s3-credentials` Secret to ensure correctness, security, and adherence to best practices.
details: Verify all Kubernetes resources (namespaces, operators, clusters, ConfigMap, Secret) are correctly deployed and configured. Check logs for errors. Confirm external services (S3/R2, Cloudflare Tunnel) are properly integrated and accessible. Validate all test strategies from previous subtasks have passed.
test_strategy: Confirm all previous subtask test strategies have been executed and passed. Perform an end-to-end connectivity test from a sample application pod to all provisioned infrastructure using the ConfigMap and Secret details to ensure full integration.
